title: alchemilla vulgaris subsp. pratensis
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris subsp. pratensis, also known as lady's mantle, is a perennial herb native to Europe and parts of Asia, characterized by its rosy-pink flowers and rounded, toothed leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe and heal wounds, reduce swelling, and promote the healing of skin conditions such as eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, lady's mantle is often used to treat menstrual cramps, heavy bleeding, and other symptoms associated with PMS, as well as to support the health of the digestive system and promote a healthy immune response. The plant's medicinal properties can be extracted through infusion, decoction, or tincture, making it a popular ingredient in herbal teas, salves, and ointments.
